title: Door Lock Failure
shortDescription: The washing machine door lock mechanism has failed to engage or release.
fullDescription: Error E01 indicates the door lock solenoid or latch mechanism is not operating correctly. The washer cannot start a cycle if the door is not securely locked, and this error prevents operation until the lock is functional.

causes.cause: Wiring harness to door lock damaged

symptoms: E01 displayed on the control panel
Door will not lock when cycle starts
Clicking sound from door lock area
Cycle does not begin after pressing start

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door firmly. Check that the door striker aligns with the lock mechanism and clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Look for clothing or debris caught in the door seal that may prevent proper closure.

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 2 minutes, then plug back in and try again.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the door lock solenoid for continuity. Replace if no continuity is found.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected to be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E01 door lock error fix

faq.q: Why does my Blomberg washer show E01?
faq.a: E01 means the door lock is not engaging properly. Check door alignment and the lock mechanism.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock?
faq.a: No, the door lock is a safety feature. Bypassing it is dangerous. Replace the faulty lock instead.

faq.q: How much does a door lock replacement cost?
faq.a: A replacement door lock assembly typically costs $25 to $55 for the part, plus labor if professionally installed.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with enough water within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error E02 appears when the water level sensor does not detect adequate water within the fill timeout period. This indicates a problem with the water supply, inlet valves, or water level sensor.
symptoms: Washer starts but does not fill with water

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the inlet hoses are not kinked, bent, or crushed. Straighten any kinks.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply. Disconnect hoses from the back of the washer. Clean the small mesh filter screens in each valve inlet with a toothbrush.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valves
fixSteps.instruction: Apply power to each inlet valve individually using a multimeter to test for proper resistance (around 500-1500 ohms). Replace if out of range.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement and you are not comfortable with electrical work.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E02 water fill error fix inlet valve

faq.q: Why is my Blomberg washer not filling with water?
faq.a: Check that the water supply faucets are open, hoses are not kinked, and the inlet valve filter screens are clean.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet valve screens?
faq.a: Turn off water, disconnect hoses, and gently clean the small mesh screens with a toothbrush under running water.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E02?
faq.a: Yes, if your home water pressure is below 20 PSI, the washer may not fill quickly enough.

title: Drain Pump Failure
shortDescription: The washer is unable to drain water within the allotted time.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the drain pump cannot empty the drum within the expected time. The washer will stop and display the error, leaving water in the drum.

causes.cause: Small items blocking the drain pump impeller

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drain filter at the front bottom of the machine. Place towels and a shallow dish underneath. Unscrew the filter cap slowly and remove debris, coins, or lint.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer out and inspect the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Disconnect and flush with water if needed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, look inside the pump housing with a flashlight. Check for small items like hair clips, coins, or fabric pieces jamming the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump is clear but not running, test the pump motor for continuity with a multimeter. Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E03 drain pump filter cleaning fix

faq.q: How do I drain water stuck in my Blomberg washer?
faq.a: Open the drain filter slowly at the bottom front of the machine with towels underneath to catch the water.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter every 1-2 months to prevent blockages.

faq.q: What items commonly block the drain pump?
faq.a: Coins, hair clips, buttons, small socks, and lint buildup are the most common culprits.

title: Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) is reporting abnormal readings.
fullDescription: Error E05 means the NTC temperature sensor is either open circuit, short circuit, or reading out of range. The washer cannot regulate water heating temperature.
symptoms: Cold wash regardless of temperature setting
Error appears at start of heating phase

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes and plug back in. Sometimes a temporary glitch can cause a false reading.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor (usually on or near the heating element). Check that the connector is secure and free of corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 10-20 kOhms. Replace if reading is infinite or zero.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, remove it from the heating element housing and install the replacement. Reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected or you are not comfortable accessing internal components.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E05 NTC temperature sensor replacement

faq.q: Can I still use the washer with E05?
faq.a: You can run cold wash cycles, but heated washes will not work properly until the sensor is replaced.

faq.q: Where is the NTC sensor located?
faq.a: The NTC temperature sensor is typically mounted in or near the heating element assembly at the bottom of the drum.

faq.q: What does NTC stand for?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. The sensor resistance decreases as temperature increases.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The heating element is not heating the water to the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates the washer's heating element has failed or the water temperature is not rising as expected during the heating phase.
symptoms: Water remains cold during heated cycles
Error appears mid-cycle during heat phase

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element at the bottom of the drum. Disconnect wires and test with a multimeter for resistance (20-30 ohms typical). Replace if open circuit.

fixSteps.title: Check for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Test between each heating element terminal and the metal body. Any continuity indicates a ground fault and the element must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wire connections to the heating element for burn marks, corrosion, or loose terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the retaining nut and pull the element out. Insert the new element, tighten the nut, and reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable accessing the heating element inside the machine.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E06 heating element replacement

faq.q: Can limescale cause E06?
faq.a: Heavy limescale can insulate the element and cause overheating or burnout. Use descaler regularly.

faq.q: Is it safe to use the washer without heating?
faq.a: You can run cold wash cycles, but the error will persist until the element is replaced.

faq.q: How long does a heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years depending on water hardness and usage frequency.

title: Motor Tacho Sensor Error
shortDescription: The motor tachometer sensor is not providing speed feedback to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E07 means the motor tachometer (speed sensor) is not sending proper signals. The control board cannot regulate drum speed without this feedback.
symptoms: Motor hums but drum does not move

fixSteps.title: Check carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor and inspect the carbon brushes. If they are less than 1cm long, they need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the tacho sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The tacho sensor is mounted on the back of the motor. Check that the magnet is securely attached and the sensor coil is intact.

fixSteps.title: Test the tacho sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the tacho sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. It should read approximately 100-200 ohms. Replace if out of range.

fixSteps.title: Replace carbon brushes or tacho sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If brushes are worn, slide in new ones. If the tacho sensor is faulty, replace the sensor assembly on the motor.

whenToCallPro: If motor replacement is needed or the control board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E07 motor tacho sensor carbon brush fix

faq.q: What are carbon brushes?
faq.a: Carbon brushes are small carbon blocks that make electrical contact with the spinning motor commutator. They wear down over time.

faq.q: How often do carbon brushes need replacement?
faq.a: Typically every 5-8 years with normal household use.

faq.q: Can I still use the washer with E07?
faq.a: No, the drum will not spin properly and the washer should not be used until repaired.

title: Motor Overload or Stall
shortDescription: The motor has stalled or is drawing excessive current.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates the motor is unable to rotate the drum or is drawing too much current, triggering the overload protection.

causes.cause: Overloaded drum with too many clothes

symptoms: Motor hums loudly but does not turn

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and remove some laundry. The drum should be no more than three-quarters full.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate the drum by hand. If it does not turn freely, a foreign object may be caught between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear of the machine and check the drive belt for wear, cracks, or slipping. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the belt and load are fine, test the motor windings with a multimeter. Replace the motor if windings are damaged.

whenToCallPro: If the motor itself has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E08 motor overload belt replacement

faq.q: How much laundry is too much?
faq.a: Fill the drum to about three-quarters full. Leave space for clothes to tumble freely.

faq.q: Can an overloaded washer damage the motor?
faq.a: Yes, repeatedly overloading can overheat and damage the motor over time.

faq.q: What causes a burning smell during E08?
faq.a: The motor or drive belt may be overheating due to excessive load or a seized drum.

title: Overheat Protection Activated
shortDescription: The washer has detected an overheating condition and shut down for safety.
fullDescription: Error E09 means the thermal protection has been triggered due to excessive water or component temperature. The machine stops to prevent damage or scalding.

causes.cause: Water temperature sensor malfunction giving false high reading
Heating element stuck on due to relay failure

symptoms: Hot steam escaping from door seal

fixSteps.title: Let the machine cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and wait at least 30 minutes for it to cool before attempting any diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the temperature sensor as described in E05 troubleshooting to ensure it is reading correctly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the heating element relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the element stays on continuously, the control board relay may be stuck closed. This requires board inspection.

fixSteps.title: Run a cold cycle test
fixSteps.instruction: After cooling, run a cold wash cycle to see if the machine operates normally without heating.

whenToCallPro: If the heating relay is stuck on, as this is a safety hazard.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E09 overheat protection fix

faq.q: Is E09 dangerous?
faq.a: E09 is a safety feature. Do not use the washer until the overheating cause is identified and fixed.

faq.q: Can I open the door during E09?
faq.a: Wait for the machine to cool down first. Hot water inside could cause scalding.

faq.q: What if the heating element stays on constantly?
faq.a: This indicates a stuck relay on the control board and is a safety concern. Call a technician.

title: Water Level Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The pressure switch or water level sensor is reporting inconsistent readings.
fullDescription: Error E10 indicates the analog pressure switch used to measure water level in the drum is malfunctioning. The washer cannot determine the correct water level.

causes.cause: Pressure switch hose blocked or disconnected
Water leaking into the pressure switch hose

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure switch hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose that runs from the bottom of the tub to the pressure switch. Ensure it is connected at both ends and not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Blow through the hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the pressure switch and blow gently through it. It should be clear. Clean any buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: With the hose connected, blow gently into the hose. You should hear the switch click. If not, the switch is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch does not click or gives erratic readings, disconnect the wiring and hose, then install a new pressure switch.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E10 water level pressure switch fix

faq.q: What does the pressure switch do?
faq.a: The pressure switch measures the water level inside the drum by sensing air pressure in a tube connected to the tub.

faq.q: Can a blocked hose cause overfilling?
faq.a: Yes, if the pressure switch cannot detect the water level, the washer may overfill or underfill.

faq.q: Is E10 a common problem?
faq.a: It is moderately common, especially in hard water areas where mineral deposits can block the pressure hose.

title: Unbalanced Load Detected
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: Error E11 appears when the washer detects excessive vibration from an unbalanced load during spinning. The machine will attempt to redistribute the load before stopping with this error.

causes.cause: Laundry bunched up on one side of the drum
Single heavy item like a blanket or rug
Washer not level on the floor

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and manually redistribute the laundry evenly around the drum. Untangle any bunched-up items.

fixSteps.title: Add or remove items
fixSteps.instruction: If washing a single heavy item, add a few towels to help balance the load. Remove excess if overloaded.

fixSteps.title: Level the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Use a spirit level on top of the machine. Adjust the leveling feet until the machine is perfectly level and does not rock.

fixSteps.title: Check shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: If balancing issues persist with normal loads, the shock absorbers may be worn. Access them from underneath and check for leaking or collapsed dampers.

videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E11 unbalanced load vibration fix

faq.q: Why does my washer keep going unbalanced?
faq.a: Common causes include single heavy items, overloading, or worn shock absorbers.

faq.q: Can an unbalanced load damage the washer?
faq.a: Yes, repeated severe vibration can damage the drum bearings, shock absorbers, and cabinet.

faq.q: How do I level my washing machine?
faq.a: Use a spirit level and adjust the front feet by turning them clockwise to lower or counterclockwise to raise.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal communication fault.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates a communication failure between the main control board and one of the sub-systems (motor controller, display board, or sensor modules).

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable or connector on control board
Power surge damaged the control board

symptoms: Display flickers or shows random characters

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. Plug back in. This can reset temporary communication glitches.

fixSteps.title: Check for EMI sources
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the washer is not near high-EMI devices like large motors, welding equipment, or unshielded electronics.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and check all ribbon cables and connectors on the main control board. Reseat any loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If connections are secure and the error persists, the control board may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the control board needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E14 control board communication error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E14?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage the control board. Use a surge protector for your washer.

faq.q: Is the control board expensive to replace?
faq.a: Control boards typically cost $80-$200 for the part, making this one of the more expensive repairs.

faq.q: Should I replace the board myself?
faq.a: Unless you have experience with appliance electronics, it is recommended to use a professional technician.

title: Overflow Protection Activated
shortDescription: The washer has detected water overflow and activated its flood protection system.
fullDescription: Error E17 indicates that the overflow or flood protection sensor has detected water in the base of the machine. This is a critical safety feature to prevent water damage to your home.

causes.cause: Overflow due to faulty water level sensor

symptoms: Machine stops and will not restart
Pump may run continuously trying to drain

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the hot and cold water faucets behind the washer to stop water flow.

fixSteps.title: Unplug the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from power for safety.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Look under and around the machine for the source of the leak. Check inlet hoses, door seal, and drain connections.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base tray
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the machine back and mop up any water in the base. The flood sensor must be dry before the machine will operate again.

whenToCallPro: If the source of the leak is not obvious or involves internal components.
videoSearchQuery: Blomberg washer E17 overflow flood protection leak fix

faq.q: Is water damage covered by warranty?
faq.a: Water damage to your home is typically not covered. The E17 error is designed to prevent this.

faq.q: Why does my washer keep leaking?
faq.a: Common causes include a worn door seal, loose hose connections, or a cracked tub.

faq.q: How do I dry the base tray sensor?
faq.a: Tilt the machine back carefully and use towels to absorb water from the base. Let it air dry completely.
